items.title: Critical Vulnerability in Amasty Extensions for Magento
items.summary: Amasty issued a massive security release for 25 extensions, with one critical vulnerability that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Unauthenticated Arbitrary File Upload in Amasty Order Attributes for Magento
items.summary: An unauthenticated arbitrary file upload flaw in Amasty Order Attributes allows attackers to write executable files to a Magento store without logging in, potentially leading to remote code execution.

items.title: Security Update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This update resolves critical and important vulnerabilities, including CVE-2025-47110, a stored cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability via server-side template injection, which could lead to security feature bypass, privilege escalation, and arbitrary code execution.

items.title: Unauthenticated PHP Object Injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer for Magento
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a full-page cache extension, allowing remote code execution via a crafted cookie.

items.title: Adobe Commerce Incorrect Authorization Vulnerability
items.summary: This critical vulnerability allows a remote, unauthenticated attacker to bypass security checks and gain unauthorized write access to the affected Adobe Commerce application.

items.title: Security Update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: Adobe released a security update resolving critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities that could lead to arbitrary code execution, arbitrary file system write, application denial-of-service, and security feature bypass.

items.title: Unrestricted File Upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce REST API
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files using a polyglot technique, potentially leading to remote code execution or account takeover.

items.title: Adobe Commerce Authorization Bypass Vulnerability
items.summary: An authorization bypass flaw in Adobe Commerce allows low-privileged attackers to bypass security features and gain unauthorized view access to data.

items.title: Critical Security Update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This critical security update addresses multiple vulnerabilities that could allow attackers to bypass security mechanisms, gain escalated privileges, execute unauthorized malicious code, and compromise store data and customer accounts.

items.title: Multiple Cross-site Scripting (Stored XSS) Vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: Several stored XSS vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce could allow for arbitrary code execution in the context of the logged-on user.

items.title: Multiple Incorrect Authorization Vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: Multiple incorrect authorization flaws in Adobe Commerce could lead to security feature bypass or privilege escalation.

items.title: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) Vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: Two Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerabilities were identified in Adobe Commerce.

items.title: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal') in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: A path traversal vulnerability in Adobe Commerce could allow an attacker to access restricted directories.

items.title: Improper Input Validation Vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: Two improper input validation vulnerabilities were found in Adobe Commerce.

items.title: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect') in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: An open redirect vulnerability in Adobe Commerce could redirect users to untrusted sites.}
